--- SOURCE 1: Professional & Educational Resources - ABCop.org ---
URL: https://www.abcop.org/individual-certification/get-certified/professional-educational-resources

SUMMARY:
The Orthotic, Prosthetic and Pedorthic profession has a strong network of organizations that support individuals and patient care facilities.

FULL CONTENT:
![Logo](/ResourcePackages/ABC/assets/dist/images/logo-abc-nav.svg "Home")

# Professional Resources

The Orthotic, Prosthetic and Pedorthic profession has a strong network of organizations that support individuals and patient care facilities providing critical care to OP&P patient populations.

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/ncope-logo-cs6-(cropped).jpg?sfvrsn=d56e7177_1)

### [National Commission on Orthotic and Prosthetic Education (NCOPE)](http://www.ncope.org/)

is a non-profit, independent accreditation agency organized and operated for scientific, educational and charitable purposes.  NCOPE works to promote education and raise the education standards of the O&P profession. They accomplish this by developing standards for and accrediting O&P programs; establishing accreditation and evaluation procedures in O&P education; and aiding in the development of new O&P programs nationwide.

​​Click to access the [**OPRESCAS** ​](http://info.liaison-intl.com/oprescas?utm_campaign=OPRESCAS&utm_source=hs_email&utm_medium=email&_hsenc=p2ANqtz-8ZckyZ2b4s8ZA0VgYeG249ktNVlrm4po-e9iHIgEgKVVbowJq5uUwO2EXb058R3IQ3iwYWTcA2dRnQasKXlBeG6dAbXQ&_hsmi=16034480)- NCOPE's Centralized Application Service for Orthotic and Prosthetic Residency Programs​.​​​

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/aaop-logo-(icon-only).png?sfvrsn=2a3bdf2b_1)

### [American Academy of Orthotists and Prosthetists (AAOP)](http://www.oandp.org/)

is a professional society dedicated to promoting professionalism and advancing the standards of patient care through education, literature,
research, advocacy and collaboration.​

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/aopa_logo_cmyk_horizontal_black.png?sfvrsn=91911b89_1)

### [American Orthotic & Prosthetic Association (AOPA)](http://www.aopanet.org/)

is a national trade association committed to providing high quality, unprecedented business services and products to O&P professionals. Through government relations efforts, AOPA works to raise awareness of the profession and impact policies that affect the future of the O&P industry. AOPA membership consists of more than 2,000 O&P patient care facilities and suppliers that manufacture, distribute, design, fabricate, fit and supervise the use of orthoses and prostheses.

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/naaop_logo.jpg?sfvrsn=40b3506f_1)

### [National Association for the Advancement of Orthotics & Prosthetics (NAAOP)](http://www.naaop.org/)

is a non-profit association dedicated to educating the public and promoting public policy that is in the interest of the O&P patient. Since 1987, NAAOP has shaped positive results in healthcare legislation and regulation through strong government relations advocacy and education of policymakers.

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/o-p-alliance-logo.jpg?sfvrsn=523c9858_1)

### [The Orthotic and Prosthetic Alliance (O&P Alliance)](https://oandpalliance.org/)

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/facilitator-images/ispo-logo-globe.png?sfvrsn=bc2537ae_1)

### [International Society for Prosthetics and Orthotics (ISPO)](https://www.ispoint.org/)

is a global multidisciplinary organization aiming to improve the quality of life for persons who may benefit from prosthetic, orthotic, mobility and assistive devices. ​​For over 40 years, ISPO – primarily through its Member Societies (MS) – has provided an effective platform for the exchange and communication on all aspects of the science, practice and education associated with the provision of prosthetic and orthotic care, rehabilitation engineering and related areas.​ ABC's Prosthetist/Orthotist certification pathway is accredited by ISPO as the USA Professional Pathway.

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/iaapoc-logo.png?sfvrsn=589bcbf2_1)

### [International African American Prosthetic & Orthotic Coalition](http://www.iaapoc.org/)

is a collaboration of practitioners promoting the O&P profession to young African Americans. The Coalition also works to keep members informed about current technologies and regulations. The IAAPOC fosters information exchange, networking and camaraderie among practitioners and technicians of African descent.

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/pfa-logo.png?sfvrsn=7edbc0f0_1)

### [Pedorthic Footcare Association (PFA)](http://www.pedorthics.org/)

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/default-album/aabcp-logo1443dd36-f79a-4e11-8a69-fc30c725dec2.jpg?sfvrsn=a26252cc_2)

### [American Association of Breast Care Professionals (AABCP)](http://www.aabcp.org/)

is a national member supported organization whose primary Mission includes raising public awareness for the needs for post mastectomy services, educating breast care practitioners and protecting against regulatory er​osion of breast cancer survivor benefits.​​​

![](https://www.abcop.org/images/default-source/facilitator-images/ew-logo.jpg?sfvrsn=9d2b1e9e_1)

### [Essentially Women (EW)](http://www.essentiallywomen.com/index.htm)

![Twitter](/ResourcePackages/ABC/assets/dist/images/twitter.svg)

--- SOURCE 2: Capstone O&P | Prosthetic Resources ---
URL: https://www.capstoneoandp.com/resources/prosthetic-resources

SUMMARY:
You can find all of our prosthetic information and resources on this page. Take a look today or contact us if you have any questions or need help.

FULL CONTENT:
![Close Icon](/images/icon-close-white.png)
![logo](/images/logo.png)

# Prosthetic Resources

![Adaptive Sports Center Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Adaptive%20Sports%20logo.jpg)

## Adaptive Sport Foundation

The Adaptive Sports Center enhances the quality of life of people with disabilities through exceptional outdoor adventure activities. The successful programs the ASC provides are inclusive to families and friends, empower our participants in their daily lives and have a positive enduring effect on self-efficacy, health, independence and overall well-being. [adaptivesports.org](https://www.adaptivesports.org/ "External link in new tab to Adaptive Sports Center website")

![American Academy of Orthotists and Prosthetists Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Academy%20logo_large.png)

## American Academy of Orthotists and Prosthetists

The American Academy of Orthotists and Prosthetists is dedicated to promoting professionalism and advancing the standards of patient care through education, literature, research, advocacy, and collaboration. [oandp.org](https://oandp.org/ "External link in new tab to AAOP website")

![ABCOP Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/ABCLOGO.png)

## American Board for Certification in Orthotics, Prosthetics & Pedorthics

The American Board for Certification in Orthotics, Prosthetics & Pedorthics (ABC) sets the highest standards for quality orthotic, prosthetic and pedorthic care. ABC certification, facility accreditation and mandatory continuing education programs represent the profession’s most rigorous professional achievements. [abcop.org](https://www.abcop.org/ "External link new tab to ABCOP Website")

![American Orthotic & Prosthetic Association Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/AOPA%20logo_new.png)

## American Orthotic Prosthetic Association

The mission of the American Orthotic and Prosthetic Association is to work for favorable treatment of the O&P business in laws, regulation and services; to help members improve their management and marketing skills; and to raise awareness and understanding of the industry and the association. [aopanet.org](https://www.aopanet.org/ "External link new tab to American Orthotic Prosthetic Association website")

![Logo for amp'd](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Amp'd%20Logo.png)

## Amp'd

Their Mission: to provide people with limb loss/difference resources that allow them to live to their fullest potential. By amputees. For amputees. Amp'd connects you to the limb loss/difference community directly. Whether you're looking to learn, become an advocate for others, or speak directly with your peers, Amp'd has the tools and services to help you achieve your goals. [www.ampdlife.org](https://www.ampdlife.org/ "External link in new tab to amp'd website")

![Amputee Blade Runners Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Amputee%20Blade%20Runners%20cropped.png)

## Amputee Blade Runners

Amputee Blade Runners was founded by two prosthetists, with the idea that giving someone one piece of equipment could change their life. Blade Runners is a nonprofit organization that helps provide free running prosthetics for amputees. Health insurance companies have declared running prosthetics as “not medically necessary” and do not cover their cost. This organization helps amputees keep an active lifestyle by providing this vital equipment free of charge. Blade Runners' goal is to provide a running prosthesis to one athlete in all 50 states.   [amputeebladerunners.com](http://amputeebladerunners.com/ "External link in new tab to Amputee Blade Runners website")

![Amputee Coalition logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/amputee-logo.png)

## Amputee Coalition

Their Mission: To reach out to and empower people affected by limb loss to achieve their full potential through education, support and advocacy, and to promote limb loss prevention. Key Tenets: *Limb Loss Is Not Uncommon, and Becoming Less Uncommon Every Day.*The Amputee Coalition works to ensure that no amputee feels alone through pre- and post-amputation and recovery. The Amputee Coalition is one of the largest amputee organizations in the U.S. and works to help amputees and their families live life to the fullest after amputation. [amputee-coalition.org](https://www.amputee-coalition.org/ "External link in new tab to Amputee Coalition website")

![Arthritis Foundation logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/arthritis%20logo.png)

## Arthritis Foundation

A voluntary health agency covering all arthritis and related conditions. [arthritis.org](https://www.arthritis.org/ "External link in new tab to Arthritis Foundation website")

![Camp No Limits Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Camp%20no%20limits.jpg)

## Camp No Limits

Camp No Limits is the only camp for young people with limb loss and their families, creating a network of support for all the campers. Camp No Limits offers therapeutic programs with specialized professionals, including physical and occupational therapists, prosthetists and adult amputees role models. [nolimitsfoundation.org](https://nolimitsfoundation.org/ "External link in new tab to Camp No Limits website")

![Challenged Athletes Foundation Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Challenged%20Athletes%20Foundation.png)

## Challenged Athletes Foundation

It is the mission of the Challenged Athletes Foundation® (CAF) to provide opportunities and support to people with physical disabilities so they can pursue active lifestyles through physical fitness and competitive athletics. CAF believes that involvement in sports at any level increases self-esteem, encourages independence and enhances quality of life.   [challengedathletes.org](https://www.challengedathletes.org/ "External link in new tab to Challenged Athletes Foundation website")

![Disabled American Veterans Charitable Trust Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/DAV%20cropped.png)

## Disabled American Veterans Charitable Services Trust

The Disabled American Veterans National Organization set up the DAV Charitable Service Trust to advance initiatives that might not fit easily into the scheme of what is traditionally offered through the VA, state veterans' programs, or the veterans' service organizations.  [cst.dav.org](https://cst.dav.org/ "External link in new tab to DAV website")

![Disabled Sports USA Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/DSA.png )

## Disabled Sports USA

Providing adaptive sports opportunities for people with disabilities to develop independence, confidence and fitness through sports. [disabledsportsusa.org](https://www.moveunitedsport.org/ "External link in new tab to Disabled Sports USA website")

![The Given Limb Foundation Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/GLF.png)

## The Given Limb Foundation

The Given Limb Foundation is dedicated to improving the lives of individuals with amputations or diminished use of their limbs throughout the world. We aim to support outreach, education, research, and support for those who are facing or have had limb amputations or diminished use of their limbs, particularly our wounded military. [givenlimb.org](http://givenlimb.org/ "External link in new tab to Given Limb Foundation website")

![The Heather Abbott Foundation logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Heather%20Abbott.png )

## The Heather Abbott Foundation

The Heather Abbott Foundation helps provide customized prostheses to those who have suffered limb loss through traumatic circumstances. [heatherabbottfoundation.org](https://heatherabbottfoundation.org/ "External link in new tab to Heather Abbott Foundation website")

![Inner Wheel USA logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/IWFUS.png )

## Inner Wheel USA Foundation

Inner Wheel U.S.A. is part of an international women’s service organization. Members spread friendship, understanding, and encourage personal service throughout our nation and the world! Inner Wheel USA has a project that provides upper extremity myoelectric prosthesis to limb deficient children. [innerwheelusa.com](https://innerwheelusa.com/ "External link new tab to Inner Wheel USA website")

![International Child Amputee Network logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/I-Can%20cropped.jpg )

## International Child Amputee Network

International Child Amputee Network (I-CAN), a 501(c)(3) organization, is dedicated to promoting education, support, information, and empowerment to traumatic and congenital limb different children and their families. I-CAN is committed to bringing together these children and their families to enhance the quality of life for the children and young adults based on the concept of mentors helping families, parents helping parents, and kids helping kids. [child-amputee.net](http://child-amputee.net/ "External link new tab to International Child Amputee Network website")

![Limbs for Life Foundation logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Limbs%20for%20life.png )

## Limbs for Life Foundation

The Limbs for Life Foundation is a global nonprofit organization dedicated to providing fully-functional prosthetic care for individuals who cannot otherwise afford it and raising awareness of the challenges facing amputees. [limbsforlife.org](https://www.limbsforlife.org/ "External link in new tab to Limbs for Life Foundation website")

![National Association for the Advancement of Orthotics & Prosthetics logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/naaop_logo.jpg )

## National Association for the Advancement of Orthotics and Prosthetics

National Association for the Advancement of Orthotics & Prosthetics. Your voice for professional O&P patient care and government agency advocate. [www.naaop.org](https://naaop.us/ "External link in new tab to NAAOP website")

![NCOPE logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/ncope-logo.jpg )

## National Commission on Orthotic & Prosthetic Education

The National Commission on Orthotic and Prosthetic Education (NCOPE) is the accreditation body for the orthotics and prosthetics (O&P) profession. As such, its primary mission and obligation is to ensure educational and residency programs meet the minimum standards of quality to prepare individuals to enter the O&P profession. [www.ncope.org](https://ncope.org/ "External link in new tab to NCOPE website")

![oandp.com logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/oandp_com.jpg )

## OandP.com

OandP.com is the leading online information resource for practitioners, amputees and health professionals. This site includes peer-reviewed articles and other industry resources to increase knowledge and understanding of orthotics and prosthetics concepts and technology. [oandp.com](https://opedge.com/ "External link in new tab to oandp.com website")

![OPAF logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/oandp%20assistance%20logo.png )

## Orthotic and Prosthetic Assistance Fund

The Orthotic & Prosthetic Activities Foundation (OPAF) is a nonprofit organization, a 501(c) (3) public charity, established and incorporated in January 1995 by the former Orthotics and Prosthetics National Office. OPAF was initially dedicated to providing financial and in-kind support to the 1996 Paralympics, OPAF now generates its own initiatives through partnerships with individuals, organizations, and institutions engaged in improving the quality of life for O&P patients through health and fitness programs, and raising public awareness of physical disability. [opaffirstclinics.org](https://www.opaffirstclinics.org/ "External link in new tab to OPAF website")

![ottobock logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/ottobock%20cropped.png )

## OttoBock

Mobility for people: the name Ottobock stands for high-quality, functional and technologically outstanding products and services around the world. Not only does Ottobock set the standard for the highest quality orthotic and prosthetic components and technology, but it provides an abundance of resources for patients and practitioners. Ottobock's website and youtube channel share unique content such as strength training and modified methods for amputees and the disabled as well as in-depth technical information regarding their products and advanced techniques. [ottobockus.com](https://www.ottobockus.com/ "External link in new tab to ottobock website")

![The Prosthetic Foundation logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/prosthetic%20foundation%20logo.JPG )

## The prosthetic foundation

To provide support and access for uninsured or under-insured amputees to obtain quality prosthetics with comprehensive aftercare, and to generate public awareness for these individuals. Organization Vision: Restoring life, hope and independence through the ability to walk again. The Prosthetic Foundation is committed to ensuring that amputees not only receive a prosthesis but also receive the follow-up care that is essential in the successful outcome of regaining mobility and independence.  
[https://www.theprostheticfoundation.org/program/](https://www.theprostheticfoundation.org/program/ "External link in new tab to The Prosthetic Foundation website")

![Texas Workforce Commission Logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/TWC%20logo.jpg)

## The Texas workforce commission

The Texas Workforce Commission (TWC) provides vocational rehabilitation services and coverage for prosthetic and orthotic devices if deemed necessary as part of your return to work plan. If you are currently unemployed, plan to return to work, and are in need of an orthotic or prosthetic device to make that possible, contact TWC today to find out how they can help! [www.twc.texas.gov/jobseekers/vocational-rehabilitation-services](https://www.twc.texas.gov/jobseekers/vocational-rehabilitation-services "External link in the Texas Workforce Commission website") 

![Wound Warrior Project logo](/uploads/userfiles/files/images/Wounded%20warrior%20project%20logo.png)

## Wounded Warrior Project

Whether you are a wounded veteran looking for a new career or you are in need of an assistive device to get back to work and improve your daily life, Warriors to Work is here to help. [woundedwarriorproject.org](https://www.woundedwarriorproject.org/ "External link in new tab to Wounded Warriors Project website")

enriched_points.expanded_content: Healthcare professionals play a crucial role in the rehabilitation process, providing care, support, and adjustments to prosthetic limbs, ensuring optimal functionality and comfort for patients.
Engineers and designers innovate prosthetic technologies, focusing on biomechanics to enhance performance, comfort, and aesthetics, thus significantly improving the quality of life for amputees.
Amputees and their families seek knowledge and support regarding prosthetics, playing an active role in rehabilitation, adapting to changes, and ensuring the best outcomes for daily living.

Researchers in biomechanics study the mechanics of prosthetics, striving to advance technology and techniques that improve mobility, reduce discomfort, and promote better rehabilitation results for users.
